articles.title: Woman’s Nose Job Leads to Bizarre Side Effect
articles.description: Instagram/Olivia Dayton A social media influencer sparked concerns over her health after she shared a peculiar side effect from her nose job. Olivia Dayton, who has over 1.3 million followers across her social media sites, posted about her rhinoplasty journey on TikTok . In the video, she is shown with her head down. As she raises it to flip her hair back, liquid immediately starts to drip from her nose. Read more at The Daily Beast.

articles.title: Whey protein shortage looms as use of weight-loss drugs fuels global demand
articles.description: Price of dairy product has risen fivefold after users of GLP-1 medications advised to increase protein consumption The growing popularity of weight-loss drugs has fuelled global demand for whey protein, sparking concerns among industry experts over a potential shortage. The price of whey has risen fivefold to record levels as companies race to secure supplies amid a boom driven by growing use of GLP-1 drugs, such as Mounjaro, which often require higher protein intake to preserve muscle mass. World: Sustaining HIV Impact in MENA: Community Coalitions and Regional Coordination and Leadership - Policy Brief
articles.description: Country: World Source: Eastern Mediterranean Public Health Network Please refer to the attached file. How can the MENA region sustain progress in the HIV response amid funding constraints, service gaps, and evolving public health challenges? EMPHNET's latest policy brief, "Sustaining HIV Impact in MENA: Community Coalitions and Regional Coordination and Leadership," highlights the critical role of community-led organizations, regional collaboration, data-driven service delivery, and sustainable financing in strengthening HIV responses across the region. The brief calls for action to institutionalize community leadership, align services with epidemiological need, strengthen regional learning, and protect equitable access to HIV services to help advance progress toward ending AIDS as a public health threat. Read the policy brief and explore the recommendations: https://bit.ly/4g8fKyv

articles.title: OpenAI files for IPO - here’s what investors need to know

The move comes just days ahead of another highly anticipated IPO, from SpaceX, and with rival Anthropic also eyeing a Wall Street listing.

articles.title: AscellaHealth Establishes International Strategic Advisory Council, Enhances Global Life Sciences Support Services Amid Regulatory and Market Complexity
articles.description: DUBLIN and BERWYN, Pa., June 09, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- AscellaHealth , a global partner delivering customizable solutions to support the specialty pharmaceutical industry, today announces the formation of its international Strategic Advisory Council (ASAC), bringing together global expertise to guide Life Sciences companies in navigating complex markets and accelerating access to rare disease therapies. ASAC is established to reinforce AscellaHealth's strategic leadership across the specialty pharmaceutical value chain, shaping pivotal decisions that drive operational excellence, cost efficiency and market impact as next-generation therapies transform the global landscape. "Developing and commercialising orphan and advanced therapies has always required highly specialised, tailored expertise," states Bill Oldham, chairman and president, AscellaHealth. "With new regulatory shifts in Europe, evolving U.S. policies like MFN, and accelerating innovation in advanced therapies, companies are now operating in an increasingly complex, high-stakes environment. The launch of ASAC ensures AscellaHealth continues to provide timely, practical guidance – helping clients evaluate cross-border opportunities, optimise market entry strategies and execute on high-growth potential while keeping patient needs front and center." The council launches with inaugural member, Barry O'Dowd, with additional strategic appointments anticipated to follow as AscellaHealth expands its global advisory leadership across Europe and other key international markets. Based in Dublin, Ireland, Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: China’s North Korea outreach 'strategic maintenance', not breakthrough: Analyst
articles.description: Han Lin, Managing Director and China Country Director at The Asia Group, says Beijing’s engagement with North Korea is less about achieving a breakthrough and more about maintaining strategic stability. While Pyongyang has strengthened ties with Russia in recent years, Lin notes that China remains its most important partner and expects greater predictability in return for continued economic engagement.

articles.title: U.S. Bank Asset Management on why they upgraded their 2026 S&P 500 target

articles.title: Productivity gains from AI are still a couple of years away: Oxford Economics
articles.description: Ryan Sweet, chief global economist at Oxford Economics, says the U.S. "lucked out" as the timing of the energy price spike coincided with the tax refund season, offsetting higher prices at the pump. He also says AI will remain an inflationary force in the U.S. for the next year, but turn disinflationary in 2028 or 2029 as productivity gains become more visible.
